author: opus-changelog-41

patch.value:
## Root cause
canonicalPendingTagName preserves the unpadded `v2026.6.13` for the pre-tag pending window, but once the padded tag `v2026.06.13` exists the window heading is built from formatTagVersion(tag.name), which only stripped the leading `v` -> padded `2026.06.13`. The committed (pre-tag, unpadded) CHANGELOG then mismatches every later regeneration and changelog:check fails on every released repo at HEAD.

## Fix
1. formatTagVersion: normalize calendar (YYYY.M.D[-N]) tags to unpadded form, matching the pending heading and the pm-cli release pipeline. Non-calendar (semver) tags untouched.
2. filterItemsByTime: compare since/until at second granularity (git tag committer dates are second-precision; pm items carry ms) so an item closed at HH:MM:SS.789 stays in the release tagged at HH:MM:SS instead of resurfacing under Unreleased.

## Verification
- 100 unit tests pass (+3 new regression tests).
- pm-changelog changelog:check up to date after regen (21 historical headings normalized padded->unpadded).
- pm-beads (padded tags v2026.06.12) changelog:check fails on published version, regenerates clean with fixed build (12 headings, zero non-heading diffs).
Stabilize calendar release headings across the zero-padded tag boundary
GitHub issue #41: release headings flip from unpadded package version to zero-padded tag form once the padded git tag is pushed, breaking post-tag changelog:check fleet-wide.

formatTagVersion renders padded calendar tags as unpadded headings; filterItemsByTime compares boundaries at second granularity; new regression tests cover both; pm-changelog and pm-beads changelog:check pass with the fixed build.

message: Tracking issue #41 fix during 2026-06-13 ecosystem pass
